sessionData.markdown:
# Session summary

Anna arrived ten minutes early and seemed visibly more grounded than the previous week. We opened with a five-minute breath-anchoring exercise to settle into the room, which she reported was easier than it had been at the last session. The bulk of our hour was spent unpacking a recent conflict with her sister that had surfaced familiar patterns of self-criticism. Anna noticed, with some gentle prompting, that her inner narrator had been operating in absolutist terms throughout the week. We mapped two specific instances together and identified the underlying belief beneath each one. By the end of that exploration she was able to name the pattern without judgement, which felt like a small but meaningful shift. The room held a quieter quality in the second half of the session.

- Practice the three-part breath cycle for five minutes each morning before checking the phone.
- Write down one self-critical thought per day and reframe it in second-person voice as a gentle observation.
- Bring the journal entries to next session so we can review the patterns together.

In the closing fifteen minutes we discussed somatic awareness and Anna agreed to try a brief body-scan check-in midway through her workday on Wednesdays and Fridays. She mentioned that her shoulders had been holding tension for most of the previous week, and we spent a few minutes practicing where to direct attention when she notices it building. Anna asked whether the patterns we have been identifying might be linked to the stories her grandmother used to tell about the family, which felt like an opening worth returning to. We agreed to start there next week. She left looking lighter than when she arrived. Overall a productive and well-paced session that built on the previous two weeks of work.
